verse_specific_anchors: avyayām akīrtim — imperishable disrepute; mirroring the imperishable-Self language of 2.17 / 2.21
bhūtāni kathayiṣyanti — people will speak; the speech-propagation framing
sambhāvitasya cākīrtir — the dishonour for one held in honour; the asymmetry restriction
maraṇād atiricyate — exceeds death; the comparative framing
verdict_reason: The engineering analog anchors specifically on avyayām (imperishable) and the deliberate mirror to the imperishable-Self language of 2.17 / 2.21. This mirror is unique to 2.34 in the duty block — neither 2.33 nor 2.35 reuses avyaya in the akīrti context. The peer-network-modelling scenario with named artifacts (Kafka-to-Pulsar migration, GitHub stars, conference talks) is uniquely 2.34's: it carries both the imperishable-signal claim and the sambhāvitasya restriction. Other verses in the block cannot be force-fit: 2.33 names the if-then loss-trio without the avyaya mirror; 2.35 names the peer-witness sharpening (mahā-rathas) without the avyaya. PASS on both 7.6 and 3.5.
